title: glyceria lemmonii
intro: Glyceria lemmonii, also known as Lemmon's Mannagrass or Lemmon's Sweetgrass, is a perennial grass native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Glyceria lemmonii are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although its effectiveness is largely anecdotal and requires further scientific study.
